mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains above 5.0% for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels combined with persistently low policy rates create negative real yields and policy uncertainty, which compress equity risk premiums and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during disinflationary periods, or 5-day forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 and FEDFUNDS stays below 1.0% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates into higher corporate profits and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 150,000 signal robust labor market expansion that sustains household income and consumer spending, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 20-day returns in > 60% of months where PAYEMS growth surpasses 150,000, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for prior month revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion above trend alongside accelerating consumer prices indicates demand-pull inflationary pressure, which forces aggressive monetary tightening expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative returns when inflation remains below 3%, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices above the 2017 baseline combined with declining unemployment rates confirm a synchronized economic recovery, which reduces recession risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns during supply-constrained expansions, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge when INDPRO exceeds 100 while UNRATE remains flat or rising.
